results.title: 10 Common Pond Problems and Solutions - That Pond Guy
results.content:
### 3. Fish Illness and Stress

Problem:  
 Fish are highly sensitive to changes in their environment. Stress is often caused by poor water quality, sudden temperature fluctuations, overcrowding, or parasite infestations. Symptoms include reduced appetite, erratic swimming, lethargy, gasping at the surface, and visible sores or redness. Prolonged stress weakens the immune system, leaving fish vulnerable to bacterial infections, fungal diseases, and parasites such as ich or flukes.

Solution: [...] ### 9. Leakages or Pond Liner Damage

Problem:  
 Leaks or damaged liners cause water levels to drop, destabilizing the pond’s ecosystem. Sudden water loss can stress fish and expose aquatic plants to air, potentially harming them. Over time, even small leaks can cause significant issues by requiring constant refilling, which dilutes water quality and increases maintenance demands.

Solution: [...] ### 8. Overstocking

Problem:  
 Overstocking occurs when a pond contains more fish than its ecosystem or filtration system can support. Overstocked ponds produce excessive waste, leading to high ammonia and nitrite levels. Oxygen levels also drop as fish compete for resources, increasing stress and the likelihood of disease outbreaks. Overstocking often exacerbates existing problems, such as algae growth and poor water clarity.

Solution:

results.content:
You can prevent most pond problems by using the right materials and design from the start. That means either hiring an experienced professional to do the work for you, or investing in a quality DIY kit.

Here are the elements that we’ve found make for a low-maintenance pond:

## How to Keep Water Clear

They’re the most common complaints we hear from pond owners. Luckily, you can solve most issues with good filtration, beneficial bacteria and plants.

We feel so passionately about crystal-clear water that we’ve created an entire guide dedicated to walking you through every possible cause of – and solution to – any water clarity issue. Check it out here.

results.title: Three Common Pond and Water Feature Problems and Repairs
results.content:
## Water Movement Is Too Slow or Too Fast

If the water in your pond or waterfall moves too fast or too slow, you are using an oversized or an undersized pump, or the pump is not adjusted correctly. Some other possible causes include clogged or inaccurately sized piping and a misdirected water outlet.

A water feature repair expert in Scottsdale will get to the root cause of your problem and fix it to prevent the situation from worsening.

## Green or Colorless Pond Water

A green pond is unsightly and an eyesore. Free-floating algae cause green pond water. An increase in phosphorus and nitrate levels in the water is usually to be blamed for algae growth. [...] ...

# 3 Common Pond and Water Feature Problems and Repairs

by Ralph Biezad | Feb 15, 2021 | blog, Pond Repair

Adding a pond or water feature to your backyard is one of the most effective ways to boost its aesthetic appeal. A water feature can transform an ordinary backyard into a tranquil retreat. The sight and sound of water trigger the release of relaxing neurochemicals in the brain.

Like everything else, water features can develop problems over time. We have compiled a list of some common water feature problems and their solutions. Take a look.

## A Leaking Pond [...] ## A Leaking Pond

If you notice that the water level in your pond is going down faster than it should, there could be a leak. Some common causes of pond leaks include faulty installation, damage by animals such as cats or dogs, and plumbing problems. If you have an older pond, concrete may have developed a crack.

Leaks can occur around the banks of your pond or in the pond dam. The first step to repairing a pond leak is identifying the source. Once you have identified the source, you can make a plan for a repair.

Check the mouth on the skimmer in the area where the liners comes in contact with the biofalls or skimmer to see if there are any leaks.

## Water Movement Is Too Slow or Too Fast

•2y ago [...] Consider a bog filter for natural biological filtration, especially if you want to integrate plants. "Natural bog filtration. Let nature do what it is supposed to."

### Monitor Water Quality Diligently

   Perform regular water changes of 10-25% weekly, and always treat tap water with a dechlorinator before adding it to the pond. "Change about 10-20% of the pond water weekly to maintain optimal conditions and remove any toxins."

   Test water parameters frequently for pH, ammonia, nitrite, and nitrate levels using a reliable test kit. "Get an API Master Test kit and an additional GH/KH test and learn how to test for ammonia, nitrite, nitrate pH and KH." [...] Add beneficial bacteria to help break down fish waste and balance the pond's ecosystem. "I use beneficial bacteria once a week."

### Protect Your Koi and Pond

   Protect your pond from predators like herons and raccoons using netting or physical barriers. "I’ve also put bird netting over the top of the pond to prevent organic material from getting in and creating algae."

   Provide shade and cover with floating plants or pond-safe structures to reduce algae growth and give koi hiding spots. "Adding some plants helped as well!"
